extra.reasoning:
rec_drug_a is set true only by r1 (priority 50), which requires ALL 12 conditions (low_ejection_fraction, renal_impairment, ckd_dialysis, bradycardia, prior_gi_bleed, heart_failure, diabetes, peptic_ulcer, elderly, hyperkalemia, immunosuppressed, hepatic_impairment) to be true. No other rule touches rec_drug_a, so rec_drug_a=true ONLY in that all-12-true input; otherwise it defaults false.

For rec_drug_b: r2 (priority 50) sets it true if low_ejection_fraction; r4 (priority 90) sets it false when all 12 conditions hold. The all-12-true input is precisely the only case where rec_drug_a=true. In that input, both r2 (matches via low_ejection_fraction) and r4 (matches via all 12) target rec_drug_b. Conflict resolution is per-output highest-priority-wins: r4 priority 90 beats r2 priority 50, so rec_drug_b := false.

Therefore whenever rec_drug_a is true (the unique all-12 case), r4 forces rec_drug_b false; in every other input rec_drug_a is false. The conjunction (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b) is never satisfiable. r4 is the deliberate high-priority override guaranteeing mutual exclusion. Property holds.
